extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (priority 50), which requires all eight conditions including heart_failure. rec_drug_b is set true only by r4 (priority 50), which requires heart_failure. No other rule touches rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b, so priority/tie-break conflict resolution never overrides either output. Whenever r3 fires to make rec_drug_a true, heart_failure is necessarily true, which forces r4 to fire and set rec_drug_b true. Thus rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are both true together, violating the mutual-exclusion property. Counterexample: set the eight r3 conjuncts (heart_failure, elderly, renal_impairment, anticoagulated, bradycardia, g6pd_deficiency, seizure_history, prior_gi_bleed) true; remaining inputs false. r3 -> rec_drug_a=true, r4 -> rec_drug_b=true.
